ground_truth.title: 23 New Businesses Now Open in Downtown Phoenix
ground_truth.author: Haley Hoekstra
ground_truth.main_content:
A new wave of businesses is bringing fresh energy to Downtown Phoenix, adding even more flair to the city's ever-evolving sidewalk experience. In the past few months, 23 businesses have opened their doors, offering everything from cozy third spaces to innovative eateries, modern wellness services, and everything in between.

Flour & Thyme is Chef Ivan Jacobo's love letter to Downtown Phoenix. This new concept occupies the former space of its big sister, Anhelo Restaurant, on the ground floor of Orpheum Lofts. Guests can expect the same level of attention to detail and elegance from Anhelo, just in a more relaxed atmosphere, with a menu featuring items from the garden, sea, and land. Details: 114 W. Adams St. Suite C103.

Ricemill, a modern Asian-inspired rice bowl concept, is set to debut a new location at 201 E. Washington on March 22. This innovative fast-casual eatery offers elevated rice bowls designed to meet the demands of busy working professionals, students, and families seeking customizable, authentic flavors and health-conscious options.

New to Arizona Center, Urban Edge Cycle + Fitness is committed to providing a dynamic and energetic space for fitness enthusiasts of all levels. The studio offers a unique blend of exercise modalities with an urban twist.

Casa de Julia honors Mexico's rich culinary heritage in every dish and aroma. Founded by Yasmine Rubio, Casa de Julia is inspired by and evokes the spirit of her Grandma Julia, creating an authentic and warm experience for all visitors.

Chicago natives Michael and Bianca Dodd and their daughter, Maddie, are bringing the gourmet flavors of a fan-favorite snack from their hometown to Downtown Phoenix. Operating out of ACRES Marketplace, the Dodds make everything fresh and offer over twenty delicious popcorn flavors including cheese, Chicago mix with cheese and caramel, caramel apple, lemon pound cake, birthday cake, flaming hot BBQ, and the Maddie jerk.

Owner Racan Alhoch officially opened a brick-and-mortar location of his popular food truck Saint Pasta in the space that formerly housed The Pairing Room. The menu includes popular dishes from the food truck along with new recipes and high-quality pasta sauces made in small batches.

Industry Standard is nestled in the heart of Roosevelt Row, merging modern American and Asian fusion in a community-focused environment with exceptional hospitality.

Condesa recently opened a brand new space just 5 minutes away from Crescent Ballroom, where it operated for 13 years as Cocina 10. The new restaurant serves all the beloved favorites plus additional menu items and an expanded cocktail program.

Aroma India offers authentic Indian cuisine on the corner of 3rd and McKinley St., with a diverse menu including appetizers, desserts, and a hearty selection of vegetarian fare.

Marlee's Lemonade is a family-operated, kid-owned business that started when Marlee was just 6 years old. Now 8, she's not just selling lemonade; she's teaching other kids that they can be entrepreneurs too.

The Grind by Chic Chef offers a third space for people to work, visit, and hang out. Led by "Chic Chef" Nik Fields, this new café features curated coffee selection, locally sourced treats, and an atmosphere designed to fuel productivity and inspiration.

Generation Tux believes that life's most memorable events should involve high-quality, convenient, and affordable options for men's formalwear, with services like delivery, home try-ons, swatches, styling tips, and more.

Someburros, Arizona's favorite family-owned fast-casual Mexican restaurant, is making a grand slam debut at Chase Field just in time for the Diamondbacks' 2025 MLB season, offering fan-favorite burros, tacos, mini chimis, and exclusive ballpark-only bites.

Nail Snob, located on the ground floor of The Rey residential tower, specializes in various nail services including gel manicures, acrylic nails, waterless gel pedicures, and intricate nail art.

Junebug Coffee, originally from San Diego, is a family-owned coffee shop sharing space with Nail Snob on the ground floor of The Rey.

Element Jiu-Jitsu helps people master the art of Jiu-Jitsu or brush up on self-defense skills with classes like Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu, Kids Jiu-Jitsu, No Gi Grappling, and open mat sessions.

Ascend the staircase next to the Heard Building to find Red Light District Bar & Lounge, a new DTPHX hidden gem offering a classic speakeasy experience with craft cocktails, hookah, live DJs, and more.

Eat the Rich is a classic neighborhood watering hole with 2013 prices, offering 100% beef hot dogs, cocktails, and country music in a nostalgic dive bar atmosphere.

The Pemberton is officially reopening after a year-long hiatus. "Pemberton 2.0" offers streamlined services in various "zones" of intimate dining arrangements and gathering areas that play with multicultural themes, ranging from Italian beach club and Tokyo Alley to Dia de los Muertos.

Kanfuela Kaffé honors its Congolese roots by roasting organic specialty coffee sourced ethically from the Democratic Republic of Congo, Tanzania, and beyond.

Pilates 1st offers a balance of exercises that build strength and enhance flexibility, with instructors teaching to all levels.

Longevity Lab is dedicated to helping achieve a better quality of life through services including stretch therapy, cold plunge, and red light therapy.

Now open inside PHX arena, The Ra Ra Room is an exclusive dining destination, marking the first-ever members-only private dining club within a sports and entertainment venue.

Concepts coming soon include Sana Sana Cerveceria, Origen, Formation Brewing, Indy by Industrious Coworking Space, Tacos los Vales, and Club Contact.
with: A new wave of businesses is bringing fresh energy to Downtown Phoenix, adding even more flair to the city's ever-evolving sidewalk experience.
Ricemill, a modern Asian-inspired rice bowl concept, is set to debut a new location at 201 E. Washington on March 22.
Chicago natives Michael and Bianca Dodd and their daughter, Maddie, are bringing the gourmet flavors of a fan-favorite snack from their hometown to Downtown Phoenix.
The Pemberton is officially re-opening its doors. Their year-long hiatus was spent reimagining the space with a completely refreshed design.
The Ra Ra Room is officially open inside the PHX arena, marking the first-ever members-only private dining club within a sports and entertainment venue.

items.caption: Combining chef-curated signature bowls with a build-your-own interactive experience, Ricemill is reimagining the classic rice bowl for today's diner. (Photo Credit: Melissa Zink Photography)

Maddie B's Chicago Style Popcorn has over 20 different flavors, including cheese, Chicago mix with cheese and caramel, caramel apple, lemon pound cake, birthday cake, flaming hot BBQ, the Maddie jerk and more. (Photo Credit: Haley Hoekstra)
Condesa's menu features authentic homemade Mexican cuisine, serving brunch, lunch, dinner, happy hour, and late night eats available Tuesday - Sunday until midnight. (Photo Credit: Haley Hoekstra)
The Grind by Chic Chef is a community-centric hub for exceptional coffee and space for people to work. (Photo Credit: Haley Hoekstra)

Someburros is a locally-owned and family-operated group of restaurants featuring authentic Sonoran-style Mexican recipes passed down from generation to generation in the Vasquez Family. (Photo: Someburros)
Element Jiu-Jitsu Martial Arts School located in the Arizona Center near the AC Hotel. (Photo Credit: Element Jiu-Jitsu)
"Pemberton 2.0" allows guests to select a "zone" of choice (pictured: Tokyo Alley), open a tab with the wait staff, enjoy your dining experience, and move on to the next exotic locale.
The Ra Ra Room is officially open inside the PHX arena, marking the first-ever members-only private dining club within a sports and entertainment venue. (Photo Credit: Major Food Group)
